channel.title: The Leadership Compass by Sudarshan
channel.description: Bold ideas and honest conversations about HR, leadership, and building workplaces that work for everyone.

item.title: Strong Legs or an Easier Road: What HR Should Really Build
item.description: Every HR team faces the same choice in disguise: smooth the path for people, or build their capacity to walk hard ones. Why the best organizations choose stronger legs.

item.title: Why the Best Leaders Are Unlearning Everything They Know
item.description: The leadership playbook from 2015 is broken. In a world of AI, distributed teams, and five generations in the workforce, the leaders who thrive are the ones brave enough to start over.

item.title: The HR Tech Stack Is a Lie (And What to Build Instead)
item.description: Sixteen tools, zero integration, and a spreadsheet holding it all together. The problem is not technology - it is architecture. A three-phase roadmap for an HR stack that works.

item.title: Psychological Safety Is Not About Being Nice
item.description: The most psychologically safe teams argue more, not less. What the research actually found, the nice-team trap, and four practices that build real safety.

item.title: AI Won't Replace Managers. But Managers Who Use AI Will.
item.description: The real divide is between managers who treat AI as a capability and those who pretend it is not happening.

item.title: The Quiet Power of Leaders Who Listen More Than They Talk
item.description: In a culture that rewards the loudest voice, the most effective leaders master strategic silence.

item.title: Stop Hiring for Culture Fit. Start Hiring for Culture Add.
item.description: Culture fit became a bias laundromat. A practical framework for hiring people who add what is missing.

item.title: Your Leadership Brand Is What Happens When You Leave the Room
item.description: Reputation is built in the micro-moments your team experiences every day.

item.title: Performance Reviews Are Dead. Long Live Continuous Feedback.
item.description: Annual reviews are a relic. What the companies winning the talent war replaced them with.

item.title: How to Build a Culture Deck That Isn't Performative Nonsense
item.description: Most culture decks are aspirational fiction. How to write one that actually reflects reality.
